title: hoya curtisii subsp. collariata
intro: Hoya curtisii subsp. collariata, also known as the Hoya Collariata or Waxflower, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. It is prized for its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hoya curtisii subsp. collariata include its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a range of health benefits.
